joyful.title: The Annunciation
joyful.shortDescription: The angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she will conceive and bear the Son of God. Mary responds with faith: “Behold, I am the handmaid of the Lord; let it be done to me according to your word.”

joyful.title: The Visitation
joyful.shortDescription: Mary visits her cousin Elizabeth, who is pregnant with John the Baptist. Upon hearing Mary’s greeting, Elizabeth’s child leaps in her womb, and she proclaims Mary blessed.

joyful.title: The Nativity (Birth of Jesus)
joyful.shortDescription: Jesus is born in a stable in Bethlehem. Mary wraps Him in swaddling clothes and lays Him in a manger, as there is no room for them in the inn.

joyful.title: The Presentation in the Temple
joyful.shortDescription: Mary and Joseph present Jesus at the Temple in Jerusalem, in accordance with Jewish law. The prophet Simeon prophesies that Jesus will be a “sign of contradiction,” and a sword will pierce Mary’s soul.

joyful.title: The Finding of Jesus in the Temple
joyful.shortDescription: At age twelve, Jesus is found among the teachers in the Temple, discussing Scripture. He tells His parents, “Did you not know that I must be in my Father’s house?”

sorrowful.title: The Agony in the Garden
sorrowful.shortDescription: Jesus prays in Gethsemane, asking the Father to take the cup of suffering from Him, yet submitting to God’s will.

sorrowful.title: The Scourging at the Pillar
sorrowful.shortDescription: Jesus is brutally whipped, enduring immense physical pain in preparation for crucifixion.

sorrowful.title: The Crowning with Thorns
sorrowful.shortDescription: Mocked as a king, Jesus is crowned with thorns, spat upon, and ridiculed by soldiers.

sorrowful.title: The Carrying of the Cross
sorrowful.shortDescription: Jesus, weakened, carries His cross to Calvary, eventually aided by Simon of Cyrene.

sorrowful.title: The Crucifixion and Death of Jesus
sorrowful.shortDescription: Jesus is nailed to the cross, dies for humanity’s sins, and commends His spirit into the Father’s hands.

glorious.title: The Resurrection of Jesus Christ
glorious.shortDescription: esus rises from the dead on Easter Sunday, fulfilling His promise and proving His divinity. The empty tomb and angelic message — "He is not here, but has risen" — affirm the victory over sin and death.

glorious.title: The Ascension of Jesus into Heaven
glorious.shortDescription: Forty days after His resurrection, Jesus ascends bodily into heaven, taking His seat at the right hand of God. This marks the completion of His earthly mission and the beginning of the Church’s mission.

glorious.title: The Descent of the Holy Spirit at Pentecost
glorious.shortDescription: On the day of Pentecost, the Holy Spirit descends upon the apostles in tongues of fire, empowering them to preach the Gospel boldly. This event marks the birth of the Church.

glorious.title: The Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary
glorious.shortDescription: Mary, the Mother of God, is taken body and soul into heaven at the end of her earthly life, a privilege due to her sinless nature and unique role in salvation history.

glorious.title: The Coronation of Mary as Queen of Heaven and Earth
glorious.shortDescription: Mary is crowned Queen of Heaven and Earth, fulfilling the vision of Revelation 12:1 — "a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet, and on her head a crown of twelve stars."

luminous.title: The Baptism of Jesus in the Jordan
luminous.shortDescription: Marking the beginning of his public life, where the Holy Spirit descended and the Father declared, “This is my beloved Son.”

luminous.title: The Wedding at Cana
luminous.shortDescription: Jesus’ first public miracle, turning water into wine, revealing his glory and affirming the dignity of marriage.

luminous.title: The Proclamation of the Kingdom of God
luminous.shortDescription: Jesus calling people to repent and believe in the Gospel, announcing the nearness of God’s reign.

luminous.title: The Transfiguration
luminous.shortDescription: Jesus’ face shining like the sun and speaking with Moses and Elijah, revealing his divine glory to Peter, James, and John.

luminous.title: The Institution of the Eucharist
luminous.shortDescription: At the Last Supper, Jesus transformed bread and wine into his Body and Blood, establishing the central mystery of the Church.
